content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of length MMM, consider the following operation:


Choose an index iii (1<i<M1 \lt i \lt M1<i<M) such that Ai>Ai−1A_i \gt A_{i-1}Ai​>Ai−1​ **and** Ai>Ai+1A_i \gt A_{i+1}Ai​>Ai+1​.
Then, delete max⁡(Ai+1,Ai−1)\max(A_{i+1}, A_{i-1})max(Ai+1​,Ai−1​) from AAA.

Note that this reduces the length of AAA by 111.
For example, if A=[1,3,2,5,4]A = [1, 3, 2, 5, 4]A=[1,3,2,5,4], you can perform the operation with i=2i = 2i=2 or i=4i = 4i=4, which result in the arrays [1,3,5,4][1, 3, 5, 4][1,3,5,4] and [1,3,2,5][1, 3, 2, 5][1,3,2,5], respectively.

Let f(A)f(A)f(A) denote the minimum possible length of the final array, if you start with AAA and repeatedly perform the above operation.

For example, f([1,3,2,5,4])=3f([1, 3, 2, 5, 4]) = 3f([1,3,2,5,4])=3, since you can go [1,3,2,5,4]→[1,3,2,5]→[1,3,5][1, 3, 2, 5, 4] \to [1, 3, 2, 5] \to [1, 3, 5][1,3,2,5,4]→[1,3,2,5]→[1,3,5].

You are given an integer NNN.

For each KKK from 111 to NNN, find the number of permutations†^\dagger† PPP of {1,2,…,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,…,N} such that f(P)=Kf(P) = Kf(P)=K.

This number can be large, so print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

†^\dagger† A permutation of {1,2,…,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,…,N} is an array of length NNN that contains every integer from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN space-separated integers. The KKK-th of these integers should be the number of permutations of length NNN such that f(P)=Kf(P) = Kf(P)=K, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests doesn't exceed 500500500.
